wall_line_handles.reason: full-width line spans entire block extent, resembles a header/sill line

notes: Width-dimension annotation block (1600) on dedicated WID layer; not a physical wall.

rationale.evidence: All 53 entities sit on a single dedicated 'WID' layer, and the geometry repeats a stereotyped pattern: a full-span line, two ~4mm-gap parallel line pairs each closed by a short perpendicular tick, and small 4/8-vertex LWPOLYLINE shapes that read as arrowhead/tick markers. The 4mm gap between the paired lines is far too thin to be a wall (typical wall thickness runs 100-300mm), and the pattern repeats twice within the block at different y-offsets, consistent with a witness-line/dimension-line construct rather than a structural boundary.
rationale.rule: When a block's entities are concentrated on a single non-structural layer (here 'WID') and show a repeated thin double-line-plus-perpendicular-tick motif with sub-10mm gaps, treat it as a dimension/annotation cache rather than wall geometry, regardless of one long full-span line being present.
rationale.counterfactual: If the paired lines were 100-300mm apart and ran the full length of the block on a wall-named layer (e.g. A-WALL), this would flip to a plan/wall classification; the full-span line at y=0 is the only element ambiguous enough to keep wall_likelihood non-zero.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection gives no access to the layer's true semantic meaning or to how this block is inserted/scaled in the parent drawing, so it cannot be confirmed whether 'WID' literally denotes a width-dimension layer or something else entirely.

notes: Evacuation-opening (피난구) cross-mark symbol, not furniture or wall.

rationale.evidence: 6 of 11 entities sit on layer '피난구' (evacuation opening) and form two diagonal X-crossing line pairs (handles 1D70/1D71 and 1D74/1D75) plus closing LWPOLYLINE rectangles — the classic Korean floor-plan pictogram for a refuge-balcony break-away panel. The remaining entities on 'FUR'/'MC' are small 4-vertex rectangles, likely frame/hinge markers rather than furniture bulk.
rationale.rule: A dominant '피난구' layer combined with diagonal X-shaped line pairs is a reliable signature for an evacuation-opening symbol; classify as 심볼 even when a minority of entities sit on a furniture-like layer.
rationale.counterfactual: If the FUR-layer entities dominated entity count and the X-cross lines were absent, this would read as furniture instead of an evacuation symbol.
rationale.uncertainty: Cannot confirm from this projection whether the symbol denotes an actual openable panel or is purely a labeling pictogram, since no TEXT/attribute entity is present to disambiguate.

rationale.counterfactual: Absence of the ARC (swing indicator) would make this ambiguous with a simple wall-opening line, lowering confidence in the door classification.
